message:
feat(recorder_migration): Cx-first m5_cutover (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Follow-on to ticks 67/68/69. Fourth of 4 individual
MigrationEngine async entry points. Only `run_m0_m2` (composite)
remains.

Addition (strictly additive, asupersync-runtime-gated):

  * `MigrationEngine::m5_cutover_with_cx(cx, target, manifest,
    epoch_ms, source_path)` — Cx-first parallel of `m5_cutover`.
    Checkpoints at 2 seams:
      - pre-flight: a pre-cancelled cx returns before building
        the lifecycle marker, leaving the target completely
        untouched.
      - between marker append and the post-activation health
        check: a cancel here leaves the marker on the target
        (fsync'd) but the caller doesn't see the
        `target_healthy` field.

Cancellation-safety contract (documented): mid-cutover
cancellation means "cutover started, re-run to confirm". The
`batch_id` contains epoch_ms, so a subsequent re-attempt at a
different epoch writes a second marker — epoch-scoped
idempotency. Operators reading the target will see both markers
and can interpret the most recent one as the authoritative
cutover.

Error mapping: cancellation wraps into
`MigrationError::TargetWriteError` (same rationale as tick 68 —
preserves Result signature). Messages start with "cancelled" for
pattern-matching.

Regression test (new) `m5_cutover_with_cx_matches_legacy`:

  * Parallel of `test_m5_emits_lifecycle_marker` under the
    Cx-first path, PLUS cross-path parity assertions.
  * Runs legacy `m5_cutover` on one MockMigrationStorage and
    Cx-first `m5_cutover_with_cx` on another, both with the
    same manifest (event_count=100, last_ordinal=99,
    export_digest=0xCAFE) and same epoch_ms=1_708_000_000.
  * Asserts CutoverResult parity across all fields
    (activated_backend, migration_epoch_ms, target_healthy,
    source_retained_path).
  * Asserts BOTH targets received exactly 1 batch with exactly
    1 event, and the marker event_id, sequence, and batch_id
    are bit-for-bit identical between legacy and Cx paths.

The batch_id parity assertion is the strongest guard: if the
Cx-first path accidentally used a different consumer_id or
computed epoch_ms differently, the batch_id would diverge and
the test would fail.

Verification (local fork bypass):
  * recorder_migration:: — 55 passed / 0 failed (tick 69: 54 ->
    tick 70: 55, +1 new comprehensive parity test; all 6
    legacy m5 tests still green)
  * cargo build -p frankenterm-core --lib — clean (53.96s)
  * cargo fmt -p frankenterm-core -- --check — clean

Cx-first recorder_migration.rs progress after tick 70:
  * `m0_preflight`       ✓ (tick 67)
  * `m2_import`          ✓ (tick 68)
  * `m3_checkpoint_sync` ✓ (tick 69)
  * `m5_cutover`         ✓ (tick 70, this commit)
  * `run_m0_m2`          — pending (composite; wiring is
                         mechanical now that all 3 component
                         methods have Cx siblings)

Bead: ft-xbnl0.2.3

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
